item.title: Confession Time...
item.description: I dislike her, especially as a Coloradan. I only leave voice-mail because they never pick up. Feel free to tell her your thoughts 202-225-4761 Edit: I keep it pretty PC and cheerful toned, so I doubt my tone or words are going to be viewed as harassment (i.e. be automatically deleted). I'm very careful of that :)

item.title: UC Davis paid 175k to have this photo removed from the Internet
item.description: It?s a pic of a Univerity police officer pepper-spraying protesters. The Reddit admins took it down from r/pics. You can?t stop the signal. Most Viral Edit: Thanks all! Here?s the post that linked to the deleted post: https://www.reddit.com/r/pics/comments/o2d4fc/removed_by_reddit/ And thanks to all the commenters for the links! Here?s the ?explanation? as to why it was ?accidentally? deleted. https://www.reddit.com/r/pics/comments/o2d4fc/officer_john_pike_pepper_spraying_peaceful/h27l9p5 And the attempted scrubbing: https://www.sacbee.com/news/local/article71659992.html Best Post of the Day Edit: Wow! Thanks for all the updoots! I'm glad everyone enjoyed the post so much, and thanks to all the commenters for fleshing out the history behind the pic! (originally posted by @superanth on 2021-06-18 13:20:56)
item.dc:creator: ThisTimeLastYear

item.dc:creator: Bessity

item.title: A thing was accomplished. (Fuck you depression)
item.description:
After fighting with my depression for a while, I found something that helps me and decided I needed to facilitate my new hobby. This is part of my backyard that has never really seen a lot of use. Basically an alley that runs along the length of my house, next to the wall with my neighbor. Sadly I pulled a dumb and only took this first picture after an amount of cleaning. A lot of scattered wood, a fucked up old table, lots of plastic totes from cleaning and old gutters, (along with a number of wild beasts that call New Mexico home, none too angry) basically a lot of shit I decided I would handle "at some point" well, today is that point!
Long shot of the pathway after filling my truck with two loads of junk, also decided to pull up some creeper vines from the planter where I stood, maybe I'll fix it up and plant something next spring.
Closer view of the aftermath, I think I might need to get a power washer for that nice deep clean. I had to respike the gate at the end. It's been unopenable since we moved in years ago and honestly don't care if it opens. (There is a working gate w/sensors on the other side of the house I use.)
Dumped the trash and stopped by lowes to get some wood to finish ny project. A simple, yet effective station where I can facilitate my hobby of painting minatures.
Other side for posterity.
The bricks were reclaimed from an older project and the wood wasn't all that expensive and I repurposed the old table from the first picture. It's not much, but it works and I felt really good both about cleaning up a mess and purpose building something for my hobby. Now if it wasn't so god damn hot out here!
item.dc:creator: RolandMalius

item.title: We can all do this. It's totally free.
item.description: I'm glad you understand the power of libraries. My stuff isn't in a library for you to check out yet, but it's still free. I invite you to listen to my first scifi audiobook short, Table for One - it's on YouTube and you can enjoy it as much as you want: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wO538-Bd-cQ

item.dc:creator: revadas

item.title: Roman Ratushny always stood on the side of truth and justice.
item.description: From the first day of Maidan, he joined the protests and was among the students beaten by police. He was 15 at the moment. Roman was on the side of civil society standing against corruption. He was a leader of the initiative protecting a park Protasiv Yar in Kyiv from a real estate company. He was always on the side of Ukraine — democratic, prosperous, European country. That is why he chose to take the gun and defend the borders from the Russian invaders. First, in the battles near Kyiv, then as an intelligence officer in Trostyanets, Sumy region. It was confirmed that Roman Ratushny in his 24 died during the fight with Russian troops near Izium, Kharkiv region.
item.dc:creator: Zergcollective
